mechanism: Partition sample into latent classes via maximum-likelihood estimation over item response patterns. Model comparison via information criteria selects number of classes. Estimate class membership probabilities; assign observations to classes with highest probability. Examine relationships between class membership and external variables via logistic regression with class membership as predictor.

Encode words at shallow (graphemic) or deep (semantic) level during exposure to musical prime (happy or angry). Test recognition of study words against foils via same/different decisions. Quantify congruency effects by comparing hit rates across music-word emotion pairs, controlling for false alarms.
distribution: hit rate and false alarm rate, none

mechanism: Model dyadic interdependence with actor-partner effects: estimate direct path from individual predictor to own outcome (actor effect) and to partner's outcome (partner effect). Include mediators in extended model and estimate indirect effects via bootstrap. Constrain parameters across gender when dyads are indistinguishable.

Binary outcome (new product development) modeled via logistic regression with country fixed effects to account for nested data structure (firms within countries). Include direct effects of informal competition and interaction terms with moderators (formal competition, corruption prevalence, regulatory optimism). Estimate marginal effects to quantify moderation.

Dichotomous dictator game with between-subject framing (give vs take). Dictators allocate from fixed endowment under one frame. Compare mean allocations and distributions of allocation types (selfish, egalitarian, etc.) via non-parametric tests (Mann-Whitney) and OLS/Probit regressions with gender interactions.

Two-stage game: investment phase allocates tokens to public infrastructure via nonlinear production function (sigmoidal, threshold effect); resource extraction phase involves sequential access to common-pool resource and real-time coordination. Analyze investment and extraction patterns via hierarchical linear models accounting for individual and group nesting.
